phases.name: Forward-record likelihood + when-distribution (backend, via a shared Models projection)
phases.description: Forward-record each day's delivery predictability — likelihoodPercentage and the forecast completion-date percentiles (the when-distribution) — onto the same DeliveryMetricSnapshot row, on the same PortfolioForecastsUpdated handler. The likelihood/when KNOWLEDGE currently lives in API.DTO.DeliveryWithLikelihoodDto.FromDelivery, which the Services-layer recorder CANNOT reference (the ModuleBoundariesArchUnitTest.ServiceLayer_DoesNotDependOnApiLayer gate forbids Services->API). Per the UI-1 decision (feature-delta DELIVER upstream-issues; originally planned for Slice 3, inherited by Slice 4 since Slice 3 is deferred), extract the projection into Models first (refactor, 01-01), then the recorder delegates to it (feature, 01-02). NO migration, NO DTO change, NO FE-parser change — LikelihoodPercentage (double?) and WhenDistributionJson (string?) columns + the DeliveryMetricsHistoryPointDto.LikelihoodPercentage/WhenDistribution mapping + the FE parser were all provisioned in Slice 1.

steps.name: Extract a Models-layer delivery-metrics projection (likelihood + when-distribution) and delegate FromDelivery to it
criteria: A new Models-layer projection (e.g. Models/DeliveryMetricsProjection record + a Delivery.CalculateMetrics(params int[] percentiles) / DeliveryMetricsCalculator in Models) computes likelihoodPercentage (the least-likely feature's GetLikelhoodForDate(delivery.Date), reproducing FromDelivery's GetLeastLikelyFeature selection: features with LikelihoodPercentage >= 0, ordered, the most conservative) and, for a CALLER-SUPPLIED percentile set, the when-distribution completion-date percentiles (per percentile p: DateTime.UtcNow.Date.AddDays(leastLikelyFeature.Forecast.GetProbability(p))) using ONLY Lighthouse.Backend.Models / Models.Forecast types — no Lighthouse.Backend.API dependency. The percentile set is a PARAMETER (not hard-coded) precisely so FromDelivery can request its existing {70,85,95} while the recorder (01-02) requests its own set — this is what keeps 01-01 behaviour-preserving

DeliveryWithLikelihoodDto.FromDelivery is refactored to delegate to the shared projection passing EXACTLY {70,85,95} (its current CreateForecastDtos(70,85,95) set — DeliveryWithLikelihoodDto.cs:146) and then map the Models result onto its existing API DTOs (WhenForecastDto etc.); the DeliveryWithLikelihoodDtoTest, DeliveriesControllerTest and DeliveriesControllerIntegrationTest suites stay green UNCHANGED (byte-identical output — do NOT add a 50th percentile here; that belongs to the recorder step)
ModuleBoundariesArchUnitTest.ServiceLayer_DoesNotDependOnApiLayer stays green AND the projection is reachable from the Services layer (it lives in Models, which Services already references)
No new Gherkin scenario is added; verification is (a) the existing FromDelivery/Deliveries suites green, (b) the full backend suite + dotnet build (0 warnings) green
steps.scenario_name: Refactor verification: FromDelivery delegates to the shared Models projection with identical likelihood/when output
steps.implementation_notes: REFACTOR step (behaviour-preserving) — keep this commit separate from the 01-02 feature commit (workflow.instructions refactor-separation). UI-1 (feature-delta '## Wave: DELIVER / [WHY] Upstream Issues' + memory project-delivery-metrics-slice1-deliver-resume): ADR-049's 'recorder reuses API.DTO.DeliveryWithLikelihoodDto.FromDelivery' is INFEASIBLE because ModuleBoundariesArchUnitTest.ServiceLayer_DoesNotDependOnApiLayer (ModuleBoundariesArchUnitTest.cs:53) forbids Services->API; the metric knowledge must live in Models so BOTH the API DTO and the Services-layer recorder can delegate. Define a Models result type (record DeliveryMetricsProjection { double LikelihoodPercentage; IReadOnlyList<DeliveryWhenPercentile> WhenDistribution; ... }, DeliveryWhenPercentile = (int Percentile, DateTime ExpectedDate)) computed from delivery.Features. The Percentile is an INTEGER percentile (70/85/95) — matching WhenForecastDto.Probability (an int), NOT a 0..1 fraction (resolves reviewer B2/B3). Make the projection method take the percentile set as a parameter (e.g. CalculateMetrics(params int[] percentiles)); FromDelivery passes {70,85,95} so its CompletionDates output is byte-identical and existing tests stay green. Reproduce FromDelivery's least-likely-feature logic EXACTLY (GetLeastLikelyFeature: Where(fl => LikelihoodPercentage >= 0).OrderByDescending(...).Last(); null => likelihood 0.0, empty completionDates). When-date math mirrors WhenForecastDto: DateTime.UtcNow.Date.AddDays(forecast.GetProbability(p)). Do NOT also pull the TotalWork/DoneWork/RemainingWork/EstimatedItemCount count projection into this step (the recorder already computes those since Slice 1+2) unless it falls out trivially — keep the refactor scoped to likelihood+when to bound the diff (consolidating the counts is a NICE follow-up). FromDelivery then maps DeliveryMetricsProjection.WhenDistribution onto its CompletionDates (WhenForecactDto list) and sets LikelihoodPercentage — output byte-identical, all existing tests green. CI: immutable records / IReadOnlyList; CA1859 concrete return type on private members; S3267 prefer .Where over loop+if; S6608 index [0]/[^1] not .First()/.Last() where the collection is an IReadOnlyList; nullable enable; no decorative/structural/provenance comments; no dynamic.

steps.name: Recorder forward-records LikelihoodPercentage + WhenDistributionJson via the shared projection
criteria: The recorder, after computing the counts, delegates to the shared Models projection (01-01) requesting the snapshot percentile set {50,70,85,95} (the 50th is the recorder's own choice for the when-view spread band — the brief's '50/70/85/95 spread'; it does NOT affect FromDelivery, which still requests {70,85,95}) and sets snapshot.LikelihoodPercentage = the delivery's least-likely-feature likelihood for delivery.Date and snapshot.WhenDistributionJson = the serialized completion-date percentiles — the RED driver

FOLDED null branch (triangulation): a delivery whose features carry no usable forecast (no feature with LikelihoodPercentage >= 0 / insufficient data) records LikelihoodPercentage == null AND WhenDistributionJson == null. This is the null arm of the same projection call — it goes green the instant the null-guard is written, so it is a sibling assert in the SAME test, NOT a separate step
Re-handling the same event the same day overwrites LikelihoodPercentage and WhenDistributionJson in place (idempotent on deliveryId and recordedAt.Date), never duplicating rows

PINNED Probability-unit contract (resolves reviewer B2): WhenDistributionJson serializes each point as { Probability, ExpectedDate } where Probability is an INTEGER percentile (50/70/85/95), matching WhenForecastDto — NOT a 0..1 fraction. The recorder test deserializes the written JSON and asserts Probability == 70 (etc.), not 0.7. ALSO fix the now-inconsistent Slice-1 fixture SeedDeliveryWithEverySeriesRecorded (DeliveryMetricsHistoryReadApiIntegrationTest.cs:260-264) which seeds fractions 0.5/0.85 -> change to 50/85 so the fixture matches the recorder's real output (BodyCarriesEverySeries only asserts Count>0, so this is safe; do it in THIS step)
steps.scenario_name: The recorder forward-records the delivery's likelihood and when-distribution for the day
steps.implementation_notes: Unlike Slices 2-3 there is NO pre-scaffolded [Ignore] stub for likelihood (only HandleAsync_NotBrokenDownFeature = Slice 2, done, and HandleAsync_AfterForecastUpdate = Slice 3 ForecastHowMany, deferred) — WRITE FRESH recorder tests here. Add a fixture (e.g. SeedDeliveryWithForecastedFeature) that seeds a delivery whose feature carries a persisted WhenForecast (use SetFeatureForecasts with a known SimulationResult so GetProbability(p) and GetLikelihoodForDate(date) are DETERMINISTIC — a degenerate single-outcome distribution e.g. {30:100} makes GetProbability(any percentile)==30 (ForecastBase.cs:51) and the likelihood exact; verified by the reviewer); assert snapshot.LikelihoodPercentage == known value and the deserialized WhenDistributionJson percentiles == known dates with Probability as INTEGER percentile (assert ==70, not ==0.7). EXTEND the SnapshotView helper record (DeliveryMetricSnapshotRecordingHandlerTest.cs:426) with LikelihoodPercentage (double?) + WhenDistributionJson (string?) so the new asserts follow the existing helper pattern, not inline EF reads (reviewer nitpick). FIX the Slice-1 every-series fixture probability fractions (DeliveryMetricsHistoryReadApiIntegrationTest.cs:260-264, 0.5/0.85 -> 50/85) in this step so the read-API fixture matches the recorder's real integer-percentile output (reviewer B2). The handler runs post-forecast (PortfolioForecastsUpdated, ADR-049) so feature.Forecasts is fresh; the recorder reads delivery.Features and delegates to the 01-01 Models projection (do NOT reference API.DTO). EXTEND HandleAsync: after the existing count/estimated writes, set snapshot.LikelihoodPercentage = projection.LikelihoodPercentage (null when no usable forecast) and snapshot.WhenDistributionJson = JsonSerializer.Serialize(projection.WhenDistribution) (null when empty). Keep WhenDistributionJson a raw string? (Item 2 — no value-converter, no migration). Idempotency stays get-or-create overwrite-in-place, NEVER a =true sentinel (forecast-minimum-data-guard lesson). FOLD the null branch as a sibling assert in the same test (a no-forecast delivery records null likelihood + null when) — it cannot be an independent RED. CI: NUnit4002 Is.Null not Is.EqualTo(null); NUnit2056 Assert.EnterMultipleScope + NUnit2045 independent asserts inside it; CA1859 concrete return types on private members; S3267 LINQ where; the recorder path already cleared S2139 (log-and-swallow per the dispatcher's CA1031 contract) and CA1859 in Slices 1-2 — keep those shapes; immutable, early returns, max 2 nesting, no comments.

phases.name: Read-API coverage for the predictability series
phases.description: Assert the consolidated metrics-history endpoint distinguishes an improving delivery from a degrading one (milestone-4 scenario 3) and exposes a null predictability series before any was recorded (scenario 4). NO production code change — the DTO already maps LikelihoodPercentage + WhenDistribution and returns DeliveryDate. Milestone-4 scenario 1 (likelihood series returned) and the percentiles half of scenario 2 are ALREADY covered by the Slice-1 BodyCarriesEverySeries facet (it seeds LikelihoodPercentage=72.5 + a 2-point WhenDistributionJson and asserts both). HOWEVER scenario 2's 'the delivery target date is returned as the reference for the when view' is NOT asserted anywhere yet — the HistoryView test helper record (DeliveryMetricsHistoryReadApiIntegrationTest.cs:343) omits DeliveryDate so it is silently dropped (reviewer B1); step 02-01 closes that gap.

steps.name: Read-API: an improving delivery's when-spread narrows; a degrading delivery's widens
criteria: A delivery seeded with snapshots whose likelihood rises and whose when-distribution percentile spread (e.g. 95th-minus-50th day-gap, or the latest ExpectedDate spread) narrows across recorded days returns points from which that narrowing is observable
A delivery seeded with snapshots whose when-distribution spread widens across days returns points from which that widening is observable

The assertion compares the spread (and/or likelihood trend) across the ordered points within each delivery — the improving and degrading fixtures read distinguishably; endpoint shape and date ordering are unchanged

Closes the scenario-2 gap (reviewer B1): the response's top-level deliveryDate (the when-view target-date reference) is asserted present and equal to the seeded delivery's date — add a DeliveryDate field to the HistoryView test record (currently it only declares FirstSnapshotDate + Points, silently dropping the field) and assert it
steps.scenario_name: An improving delivery reads differently from a degrading one (and the target date is returned for the when view)
steps.implementation_notes: ADD a facet for milestone-4 scenario 3 (@real-io). Seed TWO deliveries each with >=3 dated snapshots: improving = likelihood ascending + when-distribution whose percentile spread (difference between the 50th and 95th ExpectedDate, all <= the delivery target) narrows over days; degrading = spread widening over days. GET metrics-history for each; deserialize via the existing HistoryView/HistoryPointView/WhenDistributionView helpers; assert the improving delivery's last-day spread < its first-day spread and the degrading delivery's last-day spread > its first-day spread (and/or likelihood trend direction). Reuse the existing seeding/Points helpers — extend, do not rebuild. WebApplicationFactory<Program> + real EF + EnsureDeleted/EnsureCreated. NO production code change. CI: NUnit4002; NUnit2056 + NUnit2045; CA1861 hoist any constant array literal to a private static readonly field (recurs constantly in this test project — pre-flight grep before push); S6608 indexer over .First()/.Last(); factory-fixtures validated against the production model; no comments.

steps.name: Read-API: before any predictability was recorded, the history carries no likelihood and no when-distribution
criteria: A delivery whose snapshots carry only backlog/done totals (LikelihoodPercentage null, WhenDistributionJson null) returns points where no point carries a likelihood and no point carries a when-distribution
The endpoint shape and date ordering are unchanged; only the absence of the predictability series on the historic rows is asserted
The already-recorded likelihood + when series still surface on a populated point (covered by the Slice-1 BodyCarriesEverySeries facet — not duplicated here)
steps.scenario_name: A delivery with sparse or no predictability data renders consistently with the forecast view
steps.implementation_notes: ADD a facet for milestone-4 scenario 4 (@real-io @error), mirroring the Slice-2 NoPointCarriesEstimatedItemCount facet: seed a delivery whose snapshots set TotalWork/DoneWork/RemainingWork but leave LikelihoodPercentage and WhenDistributionJson null; GET metrics-history; assert Points(body).All(p => p.LikelihoodPercentage is null) AND Points(body).All(p => p.WhenDistribution is null or empty). Add the two predicate helpers next to NoPointCarriesEstimatedItemCount. Reuse SeedDeliveryWithOnlyBacklogTotals if it already fits, else add a sibling seeder. NO production code change. CI: same family as 02-01 (NUnit4002/2056/2045, CA1861 hoist, S6608); no comments.

phases.name: DeliveryPredictabilityChart (likelihood + when views) + Metrics-tab wiring + demo/E2E
phases.description: Add the second of the three D12 charts — a new DeliveryPredictabilityChart with two first-class views: a likelihood-over-time line RAG-banded by the existing getLikelihoodLevel/ForecastLevel thresholds, and a when view (date y-axis, dashed target-date reference line, completion-date percentile spread). Wire it into the Metrics tab beside the burnup, seed demo predictability data, and prove the seam with one walking-skeleton assertion. The chart is a separate component from the burnup because likelihood is a probability axis, not a count (D12).

steps.name: DeliveryPredictabilityChart likelihood view (RAG-banded line) + sparse/empty states
criteria: A new DeliveryPredictabilityChart renders, in its likelihood view, a likelihood-over-time line from each point's likelihoodPercentage, plotting a gap (null) on points whose likelihoodPercentage is null, RAG-coloured via the existing ForecastLevel class (new ForecastLevel(probability).color / .level — thresholds <=50 Risky / <=70 Realistic / <=85 Confident / else Certain)
When no point carries a likelihoodPercentage, the chart renders the forward-only empty state consistent with the burnup (D6), not a zero line
The likelihood view reads the RAG level by constructing ForecastLevel (reuse, not a re-implemented threshold table)
steps.scenario_name: Predictability chart plots the RAG-banded likelihood-over-time line
steps.implementation_notes: NEW component modelled on DeliveryBurnupChart.tsx (MUI-X LineChart, time x-axis, Card/CardContent shell, forward-only empty state when points empty). Likelihood view: one series of point.likelihoodPercentage (null -> gap). Reuse the ForecastLevel CLASS from src/components/Common/Forecasts/ForecastLevel.ts for the RAG colour — `import { ForecastLevel }` then `const level = new ForecastLevel(point.likelihoodPercentage ?? 0); seriesColor = level.color` (this is exactly how FeatureSizeScatterPlotChart / CycleTimePercentiles colour by RAG). Do NOT import getLikelihoodLevel — it does not exist as a shared export; it is a Delivery instance method (Delivery.ts:88), not a chart helper (reviewer H1). Do NOT inline thresholds (DRY-of-knowledge). RTL test asserts BUSINESS behaviour (the likelihood series present with the expected RAG level/colour mapping from a fixture; empty state when none) — NOT 'renders without crashing'; per the dotted-line lesson the RTL test mocks LineChart, so assert the series data/level mapping objects, not pixels. TS strict, never any (narrow unknown). S3358 no nested ternaries in JSX — extract a module-level helper with early returns for the RAG-level mapping; S107 options-object if any helper crosses 3 params; UPPER_SNAKE_CASE module consts; Biome clean; mock only the DeliveryService abstraction, never fetch; no comments.

steps.name: DeliveryPredictabilityChart when view (date axis + dashed target line + percentile spread + view toggle)
criteria: A view toggle switches the chart from the likelihood view to a when view that plots one or more forecast completion-date percentile lines (default 70%; the 50/70/85/95 spread available) from each point's whenDistribution, on a DATE y-axis, per snapshot date

The when view draws a dashed reference line at the delivery target date (history.deliveryDate); a spread narrowing toward/under the target reads as firming and a widening/crossing spread reads as slipping
When no point carries a whenDistribution, the when view renders the forward-only empty state (D6); switching views never throws on sparse/empty history

The percentile lines read point.whenDistribution[i].probability as an INTEGER percentile (50/70/85/95) — the contract pinned in 01-02 — and use it directly as the line label (e.g. '70%'), with NO /100 conversion; selecting the default 70th means whenDistribution.find(p => p.probability === 70)
steps.scenario_name: The when view plots completion-date percentiles against the dashed target-date line
steps.implementation_notes: EXTEND the 03-01 component with the second view + a toggle (MUI ToggleButtonGroup or Tabs). When view: a date y-axis (scaleType time on Y, or plot ExpectedDate ms as the value with a date valueFormatter); one line per selected percentile across points (data = point.whenDistribution.find(p => p.probability === target)?.expectedDate); a ChartsReferenceLine at history.deliveryDate (dashed lineStyle). Default to the 70th percentile; expose the 50/70/85/95 spread (a set of lines or a band between the 50th and 95th). If a per-series dash is needed use the MUI-X 9.0.1 selector lesson: series id + sx '& .MuiLineChart-line[data-series="<id>"]': { strokeDasharray } (series-level lineStyle is ignored by MUI-X; ChartsReferenceLine.lineStyle DOES work for the reference line). RTL test (LineChart mocked): assert the when-view series/percentile mapping + the target reference line config + that the toggle switches series, from a fixture; assert empty state when whenDistribution absent. Watch S3776 cognitive complexity — a 2-view, 2-axis chart with per-percentile logic clears 15 easily: extract likelihoodViewConfig / whenViewConfig (and the percentile->series mapping) into named module-level helpers BEFORE the return, never inline ternaries in JSX attribute slots (S3358); S107 options-objects on any helper >3 params. TS strict, no any; Biome clean; no comments.

steps.name: Wire the predictability chart into the Metrics tab + demo-seed predictability + walking-skeleton E2E
criteria: The Metrics tab in DeliverySection renders the DeliveryPredictabilityChart alongside the DeliveryBurnupChart, fed from the same metrics-history fetch (no second request)

Demo seeding records LikelihoodPercentage and a WhenDistributionJson on the demo delivery's snapshots (ideally a visibly moving likelihood and a narrowing-or-widening spread across the seeded days) so a fresh demo instance renders both the likelihood and when views
The existing DeliveryMetrics walking-skeleton spec asserts the predictability chart is visible and its view toggle switches between the likelihood and when views
The spec runs green against the locally started app with seeded demo data
steps.scenario_name: Forecaster sees the predictability chart (likelihood + when views) on the demo delivery's Metrics tab
steps.implementation_notes: Wire DeliveryPredictabilityChart into DeliverySection's Metrics tab next to the burnup (both consume the already-fetched DeliveryMetricsHistory — DeliverySection.metrics.test.tsx asserts both charts present). EXTEND DemoDataService.SeedBurnupSnapshots (DemoDataService.cs ~:130, where EstimatedItemCount is already seeded) to also set snapshot.LikelihoodPercentage (a deterministic series that moves across the seeded days) and snapshot.WhenDistributionJson (a deterministic { Probability, ExpectedDate } set whose spread narrows or widens across days), matching the Probability-unit contract from 01-02/03-02. EXTEND the existing @walking_skeleton spec (the 'forecaster opens a delivery's Metrics tab' case) with ONE assertion that the predictability chart is visible and the toggle switches views (extend DeliveryMetricsTab POM with getters + a toggle action; ALL locators via the POM, never inline page.locator — E2E Page-Object-Model rule). Do NOT add a new spec file (E2E-minimalism); the view logic is covered by 03-01/03-02 RTL, so the E2E only proves the seam end-to-end. Drive from loadDemoScenario demo data, never live syncs; deliveries are COMMUNITY (not premium — see the Slice-1 D4 correction) so no license import is needed for this chart. RUN the spec locally (dotnet run backend + playwright) before committing — pnpm build only type-checks, not locator validity; do not punt to the user. Confirm the demo delivery (Apollo Release / Project Apollo, scenario 0) snapshot rows are the ones the Metrics tab reads.

cross_cutting.rbac: N/A new — read view; inherits the existing portfolio read path; UI gating via useRbac(); authorization via IRbacAdministrationService; no new write surface; no /my-summary fetch. The predictability chart is a COMMUNITY feature: the Slice-1 D4 CORRECTION (shipped, CI-green, commit 554f4896) dropped RequirePremium from the metrics-history endpoint + the Metrics tab — deliveries are community; only rule-based selection is premium. This is CODE-VERIFIED and authoritative; do NOT re-gate. CAVEAT (reviewer H2): wave-decisions.md D4 and feature-delta.md D4 still read 'premium gating inherited' — they were NOT back-propagated after the correction and are STALE; follow the shipped code, not those lines. A doc back-prop of D4 is a separate follow-up (defer per the docs-wait-for-confirmation rule).
cross_cutting.lighthouse_clients: N/A new — no new endpoint. The likelihood + when-distribution are additional series on the already-version-gated Slice-1 metrics-history endpoint (ADR-050); the single FEATURE_REQUIRES_SERVER_NEWER_THAN registry entry from Slice 1 already covers them. A CLI/MCP delivery-predictability command remains optional/UI-first (deferred).
cross_cutting.website: N/A as a standalone — folds under the Slice-3 flagship delivery-tracking marketing surface (which is itself deferred with Slice 3). No website action at this slice.

validation.reviewer: nw-acceptance-designer-reviewer (Sentinel, Sonnet) — NEEDS_REVISION on 2026-06-04; all 3 blockers + 2 high + 1 nitpick adjudicated and fixed; re-approved by author same day
validation.notes: Slice-4 roadmap (US-04 predictability trend, ADO #5153) generated 2026-06-04 from slice-04-likelihood-trend.md + milestone-4-predictability-trend.feature, brought forward because Slice 3 (forecast-over-time, US-03) was DEFERRED on a real blocker: the chosen ForecastHowMany computation (live HowMany@85 per team) is statistically invalid for multi-team deliveries (percentile non-additivity; no joint multi-team HowMany primitive — only joint WhenForecast + single-team HowMany). Slice 4 sidesteps it: likelihood + completion-date percentiles are already computed multi-team-correctly by DeliveryWithLikelihoodDto.FromDelivery. Code-grounded facts: (1) NO migration/DTO/parser change — all columns provisioned in Slice 1. (2) UI-1 (inherited from Slice 3): the recorder is in Services and CANNOT reference API.DTO.FromDelivery (ModuleBoundariesArchUnitTest.cs:53 is real), so 01-01 extracts the projection into Models (refactor, separate commit), 01-02 delegates. (3) No pre-scaffolded [Ignore] for likelihood — 01-02 writes fresh recorder tests; deterministic single-outcome forecast fixture (e.g. {30:100}) makes GetProbability(p)/likelihood exact (ForecastBase.cs:51). REVIEW ADJUDICATION (all accepted): B1 (scenario-2 target-date unasserted — HistoryView record drops DeliveryDate) FIXED -> 02-01 now adds a DeliveryDate field + assertion. B2 (Probability-unit inconsistency: Slice-1 fixture seeds fractions 0.5/0.85 while WhenForecastDto uses ints 70/85/95) FIXED -> PINNED to integer percentile throughout (01-01 projection, 01-02 recorder asserts ==70 not 0.7 + corrects the Slice-1 fixture to 50/85, 03-02 reads as-is no /100). B3 (01-01 said {50,70,85,95} but FromDelivery uses CreateForecastDtos(70,85,95)) FIXED -> projection takes the percentile set as a PARAMETER; FromDelivery passes {70,85,95} (byte-identical, behaviour-preserving), the recorder passes {50,70,85,95} in 01-02. H1 (getLikelihoodLevel is not a shared export — it's a Delivery method; ForecastLevel is the reusable CLASS) FIXED -> 03-01/03-02 now use new ForecastLevel(prob).color, matching the 6 other charts. H2 (community-vs-premium) ADJUDICATED -> the COMMUNITY claim is CODE-VERIFIED correct (Slice-1 dropped RequirePremium, commit 554f4896); wave-decisions.md/feature-delta D4 are STALE not back-propagated — roadmap cites the shipped code; doc back-prop deferred. Nitpick (SnapshotView lacks likelihood/when fields) FIXED -> 01-02 extends SnapshotView. Open contract from generation is now CLOSED (integer percentile pinned with assertion owners in 01-02 + 03-02). 7 steps across 3 phases.
